prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG planetary atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Warhammer 40,000 / Dark Imperium inspired grimdark far-future world map, single huge primary continent.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye imperial cartographic scan of a hostile hive world, surrounded by toxic ocean and orbital auspex frame.
Subject: one enormous primary continent with complex realistic coastline: peninsulas, gulfs, bays, capes, fjords, narrow straits, broken coastal shelves, curved shores, polluted river deltas, inland toxic seas, ash deserts, manufactorum belts, hive city mega-structures, underhive slums, promethium refinery zones, voidport complexes, ruined war zones, irradiated tundra, and gothic fortress-spires.
Style/medium: imperial sector star map fused with planetary atlas, gothic architecture aesthetic, mechanicus technical overlay, grimdark science-fantasy cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, full single continent visible with generous toxic ocean border, large high-resolution atlas feel, subtle non-text cogitator grid lines and hazard iconography.
Lighting/mood: brutal grimdark far future, industrial hellscape, toxic ochre atmosphere, holy war and Inquisition dread, every region scarred by conflict.
Color palette: blackened parchment, toxic ochre, rust red, oil-slick teal seas, ash grey, plasma glow accents, dirty brass and bone.
Materials/textures: weathered vellum, metal etched lines, auspex scan noise, engraved coastlines, gothic technical detail.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Make the coastline natural, intricate, and realistic rather than a smooth blob.
